rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendants Wilton Marshall, An Individual, And Wilton Marshall, Trustee Of The Marshall Family Trust 2003 To Plaintiffs Judicial Council Form Interogatories, Set One; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF REBECCA DWYER Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendants Wilton Marshall, An Individual, And Wilton Marshall, Trustee Of The Marshall Family Trust 2003 To Plaintiffs Judicial Council Form Interogatories, Set One; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorney is Denied. A motion to compel further responses to interrogatories requires the filing of a separate statement of items in dispute pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1345. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendants Wilton Marshall, An Individual, And Wilton Marshall, Trustee Of The Marshall Family Trust 2003 To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF REBECCA DWYER Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendants Wilton Marshall, An Individual, And Wilton Marshall, Trustee Of The Marshall Family Trust 2003 To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorney is Granted as to Requests #1, 4 -38, 40-48, 51-68, 81, 83-84, and 89-96 as Defendants' response stating "Defendant will produce any and all responsive non-privileged documents" is not a complete response as it implies that there are documents being withheld which requires a privilege log pursuant to CCP 2031.240. Granted as to Requests #2, 3, 39, 70-72, 74-76, 78, 80, and 82 as the response is not in compliance with CCP 2031.230. Granted as to as to Requests 49-50. Objections are overruled. Defendants' response stating "Defendant will produce any and all responsive non-privileged documents" is not a complete response as it implies that there are documents being withheld which requires a privilege log pursuant to CCP 2031.240. Denied as to 69, 73, 77 and 79. Defendant to serve verified responses in compliance with CCP 2031.210-240 to Requests # #1-49, 51-68, 70-72, 74-76, 78, and 80-96 and to produce documents to these requests in compliance with CCP 2031.280 within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted against Defendant and his attorney of record, Martin Glickfield, jointly and severely, in the amount of $2,880.00. Payment to be made to the Client Trust Fund of the Law Offices of Thomas W. J. Purtell within 30 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARIAN SCHOCK ET AL VS. ABIGAIL GLYNN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT ABIGAIL GLYNN, DAVIS REALTY CO., INC. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is DENIED. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARIAN SCHOCK ET AL VS. ABIGAIL GLYNN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT ABIGAIL GLYNN, DAVIS REALTY CO., INC. DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained to the cause of action for breach of contract for Plaintiff to allege terms of the contract, both written and oral, and to attach a copy of the written contract. Demurrer is sustained to the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress for Plaintiff to allege fact supporting contention that misrepresentations regarding Rothchild were made with intention of causing, or reckless disregard of the probability of causing, emotional distress. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: COURTNEY MILLER ET AL VS. RUDY CHEW ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT VALERIE CHEW DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges all causes of action. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: THIRD AND MISSION ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. ROBERT E. BELAIR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default, Vacate Judgment, And Recall Writ Of Possession
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT ROBERT BELAIR Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default, Vacate Judgment, And Recall Writ Of Possession is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Defendant to file a code-compliant answer no later than January 25, 2021 by 5:00 p.m. Proposed Answer is not code-compliant and most affirmative defenses are devoid of any factual allegations supported the elements of such defenses. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT WALDERMAR AGUILERA DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH)
